headline: Reopen day: after a long holiday weekend, US and Indian markets trade the weekend's relief — a calmer Hormuz, peace calls on Ukraine, the Adani case near dismissal — with Fed minutes Wednesday and TCS kicking off India's earnings season Thursday.
market_story: Both big markets reopen today after the long weekend — US exchanges were shut Friday for July 4th and India's over Saturday–Sunday — so the first job of the day is simply to price three days of news. Most of that news was friendly: the Strait of Hormuz (the sea gate for a fifth of the world's oil) keeps reopening and crude sits quiet and oversold near $68.78; Trump held 'constructive' peace calls with both Putin and Zelensky before tomorrow's NATO summit; and the US case against Gautam Adani moved closer to permanent dismissal. The mood-setter underneath is still the weak US June jobs report (just 57,000 jobs added, half of what was expected) — 'bad news' that markets read as good because it buries the fear of another Fed rate hike, even though sticky ~4.2% inflation means no cuts either. That mix keeps money rotating: old-economy blue-chips (the Dow) sit at a record while expensive AI-chip names lag, and some global money is treating India as the calmer harbour — India's own fear gauge is near multi-month lows (~11.8) and Gift Nifty points to a flattish, slightly soft open. The real test for India starts Thursday, when TCS opens the April–June (Q1 FY27) results season: earnings, not headlines, will decide whether the Nifty's firm close (~24,271) is backed by profits. Wednesday's Fed minutes — the first under new chair Kevin Warsh — are the week's other must-read.

board.name: S&P 500
board.read: The broad US large-cap gauge reopens today exactly where it closed Thursday — flat, with its engine split between record-setting value stocks and lagging tech.

board.name: Nasdaq
board.read: The tech-heavy index remains last week's laggard as money keeps rotating out of pricey AI-chip names; today shows whether the holiday cooled or extended that shift.

board.name: Dow Jones
board.read: Old-economy blue-chips reopen at a record — the winners of fading rate-hike fears. RSI ~70 says it's short-term 'overbought' (stretched after a fast run), so a breather is normal.

board.name: VIX (volatility)
board.read: Wall Street's 'fear gauge' went into the weekend low and easing — mid-teens means calm, lightly-hedged markets. Low VIX cuts both ways: comfort now, complacency if a shock lands.

board.name: Nifty 50
board.read: India's benchmark reopens after a firm Friday close; Gift Nifty hints at a flattish-to-soft start, with support seen near 24,150 and resistance at 24,400. Earnings season is the week's real driver.

board.name: Sensex
board.read: The 30-stock index tracked the Nifty higher into the weekend — a modest, steady up-move ahead of Q1 results.

board.name: Bank Nifty
board.read: Banks lagged slightly on Friday but the index still sits above its key averages (RSI ~62) — financials are consolidating, not breaking down.

board.name: Bitcoin
board.read: The weekend bounce faded: Bitcoin slipped back from ~$62.9k to ~$62.7k, still below its 50- and 200-day averages (a downtrend). The ~$62.4k support is the line that says whether stabilization holds.

board.name: Ether
board.read: Ethereum drifted sideways with Bitcoin — technically still weak, below both key moving averages.

board.name: Gold
board.read: Firm near $4,187: with rate-hike fears buried, the no-yield metal keeps its bid — the classic beneficiary when the Fed is stuck rather than tightening.

board.name: Crude Oil (WTI)
board.read: Quiet and deeply oversold (RSI ~29) as the Hormuz risk keeps cooling. Sub-$70 oil is a quiet daily gift for oil-importer India; peace progress on Ukraine would push the same way.

board.name: USD / INR
board.read: The rupee firmed a touch to ~95.2 per dollar as oil fears eased, but remains near record lows — pinned by foreign outflows and a Fed that isn't cutting.

themes.note: Three days of mostly friendly news gets priced at today's open: Hormuz reopening holds, Trump-Putin-Zelensky peace calls before tomorrow's NATO summit, the Adani case near dismissal, and easyJet's £5.5bn take-private. The risk: calm gauges (VIX ~16, India VIX ~11.8) leave little cushion if any of it reverses.

TCS opens the Q1 FY27 season Thursday (~Jul 9); the India–UK trade pact goes live Jul 15 and the Adani judge's deadline is Jul 13. Firm indices on domestic buying now need profits to back them; offsets are the ~95.2 rupee and Mumbai's monsoon disruption (schools shut, city under orange alert).

Weak June jobs (57k) killed the July hike fear, but sticky ~4.2% inflation blocks cuts — 'higher for longer.' Wednesday's June-meeting minutes, the first under new chair Kevin Warsh, are the week's read on how the new Fed actually thinks.

The rotation out of expensive AI names continues to hang over the Nasdaq: Nvidia reopens at $194.83, pinned between its 50-day (~$210) and 200-day (~$191) averages — that $191 line is the one that matters.
Bitcoin gave back its weekend firmness (-0.67% to ~$62.7k). The stabilization above short-term averages is being tested; a slip below ~$62.4k support would say the bounce failed.

what_to_watch.title: Reopen day: three days of news gets priced in one open
what_to_watch.gist: US and Indian markets trade today for the first time since Thursday, so the weekend's headlines all get priced at once: the Strait of Hormuz reopening keeps holding (crude quiet at $68.78), Trump held 'businesslike and constructive' peace calls with Putin and Zelensky before the NATO summit that starts tomorrow in Ankara, and the US case against Gautam Adani moved closer to dismissal. Gift Nifty (the offshore futures contract that previews India's open) points marginally lower; India's VIX fear gauge is very low at ~11.8.
what_to_watch.why_it_matters: After a holiday, the open is where pent-up buying and selling meet — gaps up or down show what the market REALLY thinks the weekend was worth. With both the US VIX (~16) and India VIX (~11.8) low, investors are positioned for calm; that magnifies the reaction if anything surprises, because few are hedged.
what_to_watch.what_it_means: Expect a quiet-to-slightly-soft start in India and a US session that tests whether the Dow's record and the tech lag both extend. For your favorites, today is the first real print since Thursday — Apple's big jump and Nvidia's slump finally get a fresh vote.
what_to_watch.watch: The first hour in both markets: whether Nifty holds 24,150 support, and whether the Dow extends its record or fades from overbought (RSI ~70).

key_terms.definition: A Nifty futures contract traded offshore around the clock — before India opens, it's the best clue to where the market will start.
When a market opens well above or below its last close because news arrived while it was shut.

what_to_watch.the_lesson: Markets can't react while they're closed — so after holidays, all the accumulated news gets priced in a single open. The gap, not the headlines, tells you what the market actually concluded.

what_to_watch.title: India's earnings season starts Thursday — TCS is the first big test
what_to_watch.gist: The April–June (Q1 FY27) results season begins this week, with IT bellwether TCS reporting around Thursday, July 9. Indian indices go in firm (Nifty ~24,271) but on domestic buying while foreigners have been selling — and two tailwinds are queued up: the US judge's July 13 deadline on permanently dismissing the Adani case, and the India–UK free-trade pact (99% of Indian exports duty-free) going live July 15.
what_to_watch.why_it_matters: Earnings are the reality check for a market running on sentiment: if Q1 profits and management commentary are strong, firm prices are justified and the rally can extend; if they disappoint, stretched valuations get exposed quickly. IT results also carry a second signal — TCS and Infosys commentary is the best read on US corporate spending, which matters when the US consumer is cooling.
what_to_watch.what_it_means: A stock-picker's fortnight: watch IT majors' deal commentary for the US-demand read, export-linked sectors (textiles, gems, engineering, autos) into the UK pact, and Adani group names into July 13. Your favorite Reliance reports later in the season — its setup (cheap, steady crude feedstock) improves if oil stays sub-$70.
what_to_watch.watch: TCS results and commentary (~Jul 9), the DoJ's Adani filing (Jul 13), and the UK pact go-live (Jul 15).

key_terms.definition: India's first fiscal quarter — April to June 2026. Indian companies report on an April–March financial year.
A company whose results are read as a signal for its whole sector or economy — TCS plays that role for Indian IT.

what_to_watch.the_lesson: Indices can run on flows and mood for months, but every quarter the results season audits the story — earnings are where hope gets marked against substance.

what_to_watch.title: Wednesday's Fed minutes — the first look inside the Warsh Fed
what_to_watch.gist: The main US macro event this week is Wednesday's release of the minutes (the detailed written record) of the June Federal Reserve meeting — the first under new chair Kevin Warsh, who has already scrapped rituals like participating in the 'dot plot' of rate forecasts. The backdrop: June payrolls grew just 57,000 (about half the expected ~115,000), the jobless rate fell to 4.2% mostly because people stopped looking for work, and inflation is still sticky near 4.2%.
what_to_watch.why_it_matters: With a new chair, markets don't yet have a feel for how this Fed reasons — the minutes are the first primary document. If they read hawkish (inflation-first), the dollar firms and rate-sensitive assets wobble; if they acknowledge the cooling job market, 'no hike' drifts toward 'maybe cuts someday,' helping stocks and gold. Either way it recalibrates the July 29 decision odds.
what_to_watch.what_it_means: For an Indian investor the channel is the dollar and flows: a hawkish read keeps the rupee pinned near 95 and foreign money cautious; a softer read eases both. For US holdings, it decides whether the Dow's record run gets fuel or a reality check.
what_to_watch.watch: Wednesday's minutes release, then jobless claims Thursday; the July 29 Fed decision is the destination.

key_terms.definition: The written record of a Federal Reserve meeting, published three weeks later — traders parse it for clues the short statement didn't give.
A chart of each Fed official's rate forecast; the new chair has opted out of it, making the minutes more important for reading intentions.

what_to_watch.the_lesson: When leadership changes at a central bank, markets re-learn its reaction function from scratch — early documents and speeches carry outsized weight because there's no track record to lean on.

what_to_watch.title: Nvidia at the line: the AI rotation meets the 200-day average
what_to_watch.gist: Nvidia reopens at $194.83, rated Strong Sell short-term and caught between its 50-day (~$210) and 200-day (~$191) averages after money rotated out of expensive AI-chip names into value. The AI-expectations reset that fed the move — Meta's layoffs, Goldman's job-loss estimates, Yann LeCun's 'AI is not smart' — hasn't gone away over the weekend.
what_to_watch.why_it_matters: The 200-day average (~$191) is the market's rough dividing line between 'a pullback inside a long-term uptrend' and 'a broken trend.' Nvidia is the AI trade's flagship: if it holds $191 and stabilises, the rotation reads as healthy rebalancing; a decisive close below it would tell you big money is genuinely repricing the AI build-out — and that would ripple through the whole Nasdaq and India's IT names too.
what_to_watch.what_it_means: If you hold Nvidia (or high-growth tech generally), this week is about levels, not stories: $191 is the line that flips the technical picture, $210 is what bulls must reclaim. Expect two-way volatility — this is a beta-2.2 stock (it swings about twice the market's move).
what_to_watch.watch: Whether NVDA closes below ~$191 (trend break) or reclaims ~$210 (pullback ending).

key_terms.definition: The average closing price over the last 200 trading days — the most-watched line between a long-term uptrend (price above) and downtrend (price below).
How much a stock moves relative to the market; beta 2 means roughly double the market's swings, in both directions.

what_to_watch.the_lesson: In momentum stocks, widely-watched levels become self-fulfilling — so many investors key off the 200-day average that the reaction AT the level often matters more than the news that got the price there.

what_to_watch.title: Bitcoin's bounce is fading — $62.4k support is the test
what_to_watch.gist: Bitcoin slipped 0.67% to ~$62,669, giving back the modest gains it built over the weekend (~$62,868 on Sunday). It still trades below its 50-day (~$66,700) and 200-day (~$74,700) averages — the definition of a downtrend — with momentum neutral (RSI ~48) and price near the bottom tenth of its 52-week range after the long slide from ~$114k.
what_to_watch.why_it_matters: Last week's story was 'early stabilization': Bitcoin holding above its short-term averages for the first time in weeks. Giving those gains back doesn't kill that story, but it moves the test forward: ~$62.4k is the support that has held the recent floor. Hold it, and the base-building continues; lose it, and the downtrend resumes toward fresh lows — a sentiment read for risk appetite generally, since crypto is the purest 'risk-on/risk-off' gauge trading 24/7.
what_to_watch.what_it_means: For a Bitcoin holder, nothing about the bigger downtrend has changed — the ~$66.7k 50-day average is still the level that would signal a genuine turn. Short term, watch whether US markets reopening adds buyers or sellers.
what_to_watch.watch: The ~$62.4k support level; then the 50-day average near ~$66.7k as the trend-change trigger.

key_terms.definition: A price level where buying has repeatedly stopped declines; a decisive break below it often invites more selling.
Shorthand for markets' appetite mode: risk-on means investors chase growth assets like crypto; risk-off means they flee to safety.

what_to_watch.the_lesson: Bottoming is a process, not an event — early stabilization attempts often fail once or twice at support before a real base forms, which is why the trend-defining averages matter more than any single bounce.

global_summary.us: US markets reopen today after the July 4th holiday at last Thursday's closes: S&P 500 ~7,483 (flat), Dow at a record ~52,900 (+1.14%, RSI ~70 overbought), Nasdaq ~25,833 (-0.8%), VIX low at ~16. The tape is split — value leading, AI-chips lagging — on a weak jobs report (57k) that killed rate-hike fears. This week: June Fed minutes Wednesday (first under chair Warsh), jobless claims, and early Q2 reports (Delta, PepsiCo).
global_summary.india: Indian markets reopen after the weekend from a firm Friday close — Nifty ~24,271 (+0.39%), Sensex ~77,764 (+0.34%), Bank Nifty ~57,939 (-0.16%) — with Gift Nifty hinting flat-to-soft and India VIX very low (~11.8). The week turns on Q1 earnings (TCS ~Jul 9), the Adani filing (Jul 13) and the UK trade pact (Jul 15); Mumbai's monsoon disruption and the ~95.2 rupee are the local frictions.
global_summary.crypto: Bitcoin faded to ~$62,669 (-0.67%), giving back the weekend's gains and testing its ~$62.4k support inside a downtrend (below 50- and 200-day averages); Ether flat at ~$1,778. Stabilization is on probation, not confirmed.
global_summary.macro: Crude quiet and oversold at $68.78 as the Hormuz reopening holds and Ukraine peace calls add downside pressure; gold firm near $4,187 on the no-hikes backdrop; rupee ~95.2. Key macro dates: NATO summit Jul 7–8, Fed minutes Jul 8 (US time), Fed decision Jul 29.

catalysts_today.event: US and Indian markets REOPEN after the long holiday weekend — first pricing of Hormuz, Ukraine-peace and Adani news
PM Modi begins Indo-Pacific tour in Indonesia (then Australia summit Jul 8-10, New Zealand Jul 10-11)
Super Typhoon Bavi (Cat 5) crosses Guam/Marianas; forecast track approaches Taiwan ~Jul 11 — chip-supply watch item
Paris appeals-court verdict on Marine Le Pen's eligibility — French bonds/euro sensitivity; NATO summit opens in Ankara (Jul 7-8)
June Fed meeting minutes — first under new chair Kevin Warsh
TCS Q1 FY27 results — kicks off India's earnings season
Early US Q2 earnings: Delta Air Lines, PepsiCo
US DoJ deadline to justify permanently dismissing the Adani case
India–UK free-trade pact (CETA) comes into force — 99% of Indian exports duty-free

favorites.name: Apple Inc.
favorites.whats_new: Today Apple finally trades again after three days shut — the first real vote on whether Thursday's ~4.8% surge (foldable-iPhone and cheaper-memory-chip reports) holds. It reopens at $308.63, in a confirmed uptrend near the top of its 52-week range, but short-term gauges (stochastic, Williams %R) flag 'overbought,' so a pause near the $314 resistance wouldn't be a surprise. It remains the strongest chart in the favorites book.

news.headline: Apple's stock recovers after hit from unprecedented price hikes on products

favorites.fundamentals: Mega-cap tech, ~$4.53T market cap. Trailing P/E ~37 (expensive — you're paying up for growth), forward P/E ~32 (profits expected to grow). Revenue growing ~17% a year, keeping ~27¢ of every sales dollar as profit. Beta ~1.10 (about as jumpy as the market).
favorites.technicals: Strong Buy. Price ($308.63) is above both its 50-day (~$294) and 200-day (~$271) averages — the classic healthy-uptrend shape — at the 94th percentile of its 52-week range ($202–$315). RSI ~60 is neutral (room to run), but stochastic and Williams %R are overbought, so short-term chop is likely. Resistance $314, then $320; support $298, and a close below the 50-day (~$294) would warn the trend is stalling.
favorites.catalysts: Today's reopen (does the foldable-iPhone pop hold?); confirmation of component orders; next quarterly earnings and AI/services commentary.
favorites.risks: Rich valuation leaves no room for a miss; it's priced near record highs on reports, not confirmed numbers — and a broader tech pullback or a fade of the AI-rotation calm could drag it despite its own strength.

favorites.name: Nvidia
favorites.whats_new: Reopens today at $194.83 after the long weekend, still in the AI-chip penalty box — and now just ~$4 above the level that matters most: its 200-day average (~$191). The weekend added no relief for the AI narrative (the expectations-reset drumbeat continues), so today-to-Friday is effectively a public test of that $191 line while the rotation to value plays out.

news.headline: AI is 'not smart' — what's next in artificial intelligence?
Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Monday

favorites.fundamentals: ~$4.72T market cap with exceptional ~63¢-per-dollar profit margins. Trailing P/E ~30, forward P/E ~15 — the market expects profits to keep compounding fast (revenue +85% a year). Beta ~2.21: it swings roughly twice as hard as the market, both ways.
favorites.technicals: Strong Sell (short-term). Price ($194.83) is below its 50-day (~$210) but above its 200-day (~$191) — a transitional zone that breaks one way or the other. RSI ~41 is weak but not oversold; MACD momentum negative. The line in the sand: hold ~$191 (200-day) and the long-term uptrend survives; close below it and the trend is broken. Reclaiming ~$210 would be the first genuine repair signal.
favorites.catalysts: This week's tape action at the $191 level; next earnings and cloud-capex guidance — the real referendum on AI spending.
favorites.risks: A high-beta momentum name in a rotation against it; if AI-valuation doubts deepen or the 200-day breaks, drawdowns can be fast. Watch Typhoon Bavi's Taiwan track late-week as a tail-risk for chip supply chains.

favorites.name: Reliance Industries
favorites.whats_new: Reopens today at ₹1,304 into the most supportive backdrop in weeks: Hormuz keeps normalising, India's gas emergency stands lifted, and crude is steady sub-$70 — all good for its oil-to-chemicals feedstock costs. The Adani-case wind-down also lifts the broader India large-cap mood. But the chart hasn't turned: still below both key averages, drifting near the bottom of its 52-week range, waiting for Q1 earnings season as the real catalyst.

news.headline: India eyes oil exploration expansion after Middle East war shortages
Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Monday

favorites.fundamentals: ~₹17.66T market cap, energy-heavy conglomerate (refining, petrochemicals, Jio telecom, retail). Trailing P/E ~22 / forward ~18 (moderate), revenue +12.5% a year, ~8¢ of every sales rupee kept as profit. Beta ~0.18 — far steadier than the market.
favorites.technicals: Strong Sell, but a drift rather than a plunge. Price (₹1,304) is below the 50-day (~₹1,343) and 200-day (~₹1,419) averages — a textbook downtrend — near the 14th percentile of its 52-week range (₹1,259–₹1,592), where value hunters watch. RSI ~46 neutral. Reclaiming ~₹1,343 is what would shift the near-term picture; support sits ~₹1,300 then the ₹1,259 52-week low.
favorites.catalysts: Q1 FY27 results this season (refining margins with cheap steady crude; Jio and retail momentum); any India-market re-rating on the earnings/trade-pact/Adani trio.
favorites.risks: The downtrend is intact until ₹1,343 is reclaimed; a renewed oil spike (Hormuz 'fees' clash, Ukraine escalation) would squeeze refining economics, and a weak Q1 would extend the drift.

favorites.name: Bitcoin
favorites.whats_new: The only favorite that traded through the weekend — and it gave the bounce back: down 0.67% to ~$62,669 from Sunday's ~$62,868, wiping out the weekend's firmness. The 'early stabilization' story is now on probation: the ~$62.4k support underneath is the test, and the downtrend (price below the ~$66.7k 50-day and ~$74.7k 200-day averages) is still the ruling trend.

news.headline: Bitcoin zooms above $61,000 as inflation fears soften

favorites.fundamentals: ~$1.26T total market value; sits near the bottom tenth of its 52-week range after the slide from ~$114k. No cash flows — price is set by flows, halving-cycle sentiment and risk appetite.
favorites.technicals: Neutral rating, weakening at the margin. Price (~$62,669) is below both the 50-day (~$66,736) and 200-day (~$74,709) averages — a confirmed downtrend — with RSI ~48 (neutral) and the recent floor at ~$62.4k. Hold that support and the base-building continues; lose it and the path toward the ~$58.6k 52-week low reopens. A close above ~$66.7k is what would signal a real turn.
favorites.catalysts: US markets reopening (risk appetite read-through), ETF flow data, and the Fed-minutes tone Wednesday — crypto is the purest rates-and-liquidity trade on the board.
favorites.risks: Downtrend intact; a break of ~$62.4k invites momentum selling. As the 24/7 risk gauge, it would also be first to show any weekend-news disappointment.

risks: Complacency into the reopen: with the US VIX ~16 and India VIX ~11.8, almost no one is hedged — any negative surprise (a Hormuz 'fees' clash, a NATO-summit failure, an Iran succession shock) lands on an unprepared market.
The Fed minutes are an unknown: a hawkish first document from new chair Warsh could firm the dollar, pressure the ~95.2 rupee, and unwind the Dow's overbought record run.
Earnings risk in India: firm indices rest on domestic buying while foreigners sell; a weak TCS print Thursday would hit IT and puncture the 'India as calm harbour' narrative.
AI-valuation reset isn't finished: Nvidia sits ~$4 above its 200-day average — a break of ~$191 would ripple through the Nasdaq, high-beta tech and Indian IT sentiment.
Weather as a market wildcard: Mumbai's monsoon disruption is a food-inflation watch item, and Typhoon Bavi's forecast track toward Taiwan (~Jul 11) is a tail-risk for chip supply chains.
Crypto support test: a Bitcoin close below ~$62.4k would end the stabilization attempt and dent broader risk appetite.
disclaimer: For information only. Not financial advice. Prices are from free, possibly-delayed sources (yfinance/CoinGecko) and reflect the last close before today's reopen; verify before acting.
